title: FOIA J. Burney - US Army - Vietnam War

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Name of Veteran: Jerry L. Burney
Branch: United States Army
DOB: March 6, 1944
Dates of Service: 1965 to 1988

Please provide the following military service information:
1. dates of service, dates of ranks, stations, list of awards, decorations, Badges, assignments, Vietnam War service
2. Section VII CURRENT AND PREVIOUS ASSIGNMENTS form
3. copy of Bronze Star Medal award citation
4. Military Education/ training courses
5. copy of Defense Meritorious Service Medal award citation
6. request copy of Army commendation award citations
7. Section V MISCELLANEOUS Form

Historical Documentation
military career include Army Commendation Medal, Republic of Vietnam Gallantry Cross Unit Citation Ribbon, Vietnam Service Medal.

First name: Jerry
Last name: Burney
DOB - 3/6/1944

I request hard copies of paperwork in the file showing this information. 
Please mail hardcopies to:

MUCKROCK NEWS
263 HUNTINGTON AVE
BOSTON, MA 02115

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: FOIA  J. Robb - US Navy - TOPGUN  Naval Aviator

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) for James A. Robb
Request copy of history of assignments NAVPERS form 1070/605, Legion of Merit award citations, Meritorious Service medal citation, Navy Commendation medal citations, navy achievement medal citations, Air Medal award citations, all award citations, Cumulative Record of Training (NAVPERS Form 5300/16), Transfers and Receipts NAVPERS 601-12 from 1970 to 2000, Administrative remarks NAVPERS 1070/613, all dates of promotions.
Please redact any personal information per Freedom of Information Act, U.S.C. § 552(b)(3) and U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).

VETERAN INFORMATION
Name of veteran: James Robb
Branch of service: United States Navy
Rank: CAPT
Service: 1969 to 2006

First Name - James
Middle Initial - A
Last name - Robb

Passed away Nov 1, 2023
obituary https://www.jonesfh.org/obituary/radm-james-robb

Aviation Commands:
Fighter Squadron ONE TWO FOUR (VF-124) “Gunfighters”
Fighter Squadron ONE ZERO ONE (VF-101)
Fighter Squadron THREE TWO (VF-32) 
Executive Officer of Fighter Squadron FIVE ONE (VF-51)
Commanding Officer of VF-51
Deputy Commander (DCAG) of Carrier Air Wing ELEVEN (CVW-11)
Navy Fighter Weapons School (TOPGUN)
Carrier Air Wing NINE (CVW-9)
Carrier Group SEVEN (COMCARGRU 7)

Defense Distinguished Service Medal, Defense Superior Service Medal, Legion of Merit (four awards,) Defense Meritorious Service Medal, Meritorious Service Medal (three awards,) Air Medal with Numeral “2,” Navy Commendation Medal, Navy Achievement Medal

Combat Tours:
Southern Watch: CAG-9 Jan-June 1993, USS Nimitz
 (F-14, A-6, S-3)
Enduring Freedom: USCENTCOM, Dec 2001-March 2004
Total Combat Missions: 40 (F-14, A-6)

https://www.epnaao.com/BIOS_files/ALL_MEMBERS/Robb-James.pdf

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Eric Bradley

title: Records Related to the Death of James Pruitt (July 4, 2003) and Associated Individuals

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all records related to the investigation, prosecution, and underlying incident involving the death of James Pruitt on July 4th, 2003. This request encompasses records created or maintained from July 4, 2003, through the present, including records maintained under any case number or file associated with this matter.

This requests includes, but is not limited to: police reports, incident reports, investigative files, witness statements, recorded interviews, interrogation recordings, forensic reports, autopsy reports, evidence logs, crime scene photographs, charging documents, indictments, plea agreements, deposition transcripts, trial transcripts, sentencing and probation hearing transcripts, exhibits, and any correspondence related to the investigation or prosecution of this matter.

Please include any records that reference, mention, or are otherwise associated with the individuals listed below or the incident described, even if those individuals were not ultimately charged or were charged separately, or appear in records related to co-defendants or related investigations:

Lucille Faith Duncan (DOB: 09/10/1966)
Gerald Alan Duncan (DOB: 02/23/1961)
Jeremiah William Fredrick Robertson (DOB: 03/18/1989)
James Pruitt (deceased, DOB: 09/1958)

I request that the records be provided in electronic format (PDF) if available.

If there are any fees associated with this request, please inform me in advance if the total will exceed $100. I respectfully request a waiver of all fees, as this request is made in the public interest and not for commercial purposes.

If any portion of this request is denied, please provide a written explanation for the denial, including the specific exemption(s) relied upon.

Please contact me at ezc6@georgetown.edu and/or (571) 205-2797 if you have any questions or need clarification.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Marc Howard

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Case file and final case summary/most recent status report related to two home invasions that occurred on July 3 and July 15, 1996 at the same home in Manchester. These incidents involved multiple Asian males breaking into the home, tying up occupants and robbing them at gunpoint. Court records from Hillsborough North show charges brought by Manchester PD against one individual - Wei Chen- which are attached, along with a newspaper detailing the incident.

2. All police reports, incident reports, and arrest records pertaining to the July 15, 1996 arrest of Wei Chen (DOB: 10/01/1979) on charges of conspiracy (Hillsborough North Case No. 216-1996-CR-01300). 

Please include all documents that detail the final or most recent status of the investigation, witness statements, and the disposition of the charges against these individuals. I am happy to work with your office to shorten the scope of the request in the interest of saving everyone time. I am primarily seeking case summary documents, case outcomes (arrests/prosecutions/convictions/sentences), and related intelligence reports concerning New York and Massachusetts based Asian gangs related to this series of crimes.

I understand that some information may be exempt from disclosure under RSA 91-A. I request that any such exempt information be redacted, and the remainder of the records be provided. Please provide a detailed explanation for any redactions or denials of access.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Peter Barth

title: Full List of Custodians of Records for City of St Louis

communications.communication:
March 27, 2026
 
City of St. Louis
City Counselor’s Office
1520 Market Street
Room 4025
St. Louis, MO 63103
 
Custodian of Records/ Chief Legal Counsel,

This is a request for records under the Missouri Sunshine Law, Chapter 610, Revised Statutes of Missouri.

Section 610.023.1, RSMo, provides that a public governmental body is to appoint a custodian to maintain that body’s records and the identity and location of the custodian is to be made available upon request; and
Section 610.026, RSMo, sets forth that a public governmental body shall provide access to and, upon request, furnish copies of public records; and
Section 610.028.2, RSMo, provides that a public governmental body shall provide a reasonable written policy in compliance with sections 610.010 to 610.030, RSMo, commonly referred to as the Sunshine Law, regarding the release of information on any meeting, record or vote.
 
Requested Documents/Records:

I request that you provide copies of:

All documents/records sufficient to clearly identify the names and locations of the most current appointed custodians of records for all agencies, departments, sections, boards and commissions of the City of St Louis, Missouri.

Please include the custodian’s first/last name, title, physical location with room/suite, street address (for postal service), city, state, email address and direct phone number with extension.

I am respectfully seeking all documents/records through MuckRock.com.

I am requesting that documents/records be sent electronically, preferably as a searchable PDF file attachment or any other digital formats in which they exist (such a CSV or Excel).

If you are unable to fill the request electronically, please advise me of other alternatives.
 
Fee Waiver/Public Interest/Public Release:

As the records sought detail the operations and activities of local government, I also request that fees be waived as this request is in the public’s interest. I am seeking the records to analyze, inform and freely release to members of the public as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com.

As stated in Section 610.022, RSMo, “public records shall be presumed to be open…documents may be furnished without charge or at a reduced charge when the public governmental body determines that waiver or reduction of the fee is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to the public understanding of the operations or activities of the public governmental body and is not primarily in the commercial interest of the requester.”

The subject of the requested documents/records will provide significant insight into local governmental operations and activities around access to public records/documents. The requested records are not for commercial use.

If fees cannot be waived, I am willing to pay all reasonable duplication expenses incurred in the processing this Missouri Sunshine Law request. If fees cannot be waived, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of completing fulfilment of my request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kevin Hawkins
